props.heading: Your Next Adventure Starts Here
props.subheading: Hikers, kayakers & climbers — find your trail, own your water, and conquer every summit.
props.imageAlt: A group of hikers exploring a mountain trail

props.title: Hikers, Kayakers & Climbers
props.body:
# Trail Blazers
For those who find their rhythm on rugged terrain, hiking is more than a hobby — it's a calling. From gentle woodland paths to grueling alpine ascents, hikers push their limits with every step. Feel the crunch of gravel underfoot, breathe in crisp mountain air, and earn those panoramic summit views that no photograph can truly capture.

# Paddle Warriors
Kayakers know that the water is alive — always moving, always challenging. Whether you're carving through whitewater rapids or gliding silently across a glassy lake at dawn, kayaking demands focus, strength, and a deep respect for nature. Every paddle stroke carries you further into the wild, where the only sounds are rushing water and your own heartbeat.

# Summit Seekers
Climbers live for the vertical world — where every handhold is a decision and every ascent is a test of will. Whether bouldering at ground level, sport climbing sheer rock faces, or tackling a multi-day alpine route, the mountain rewards those who dare to look up. The summit is just the destination; the climb is the whole point.

props.heading: Guided Hikes That Push You Further
props.body:
Our expert guides know every switchback, summit, and shortcut. Whether you're tackling your first peak or chasing a personal best, they'll pace you right and keep you safe. Small groups. Big rewards. Real adventure.

All skill levels welcome. No experience needed — just the drive to get out there.

props.heading: Your Guide to the Summit
props.body:
Every great ascent starts with the right team behind you. Our experienced guides know these peaks inside out — the routes, the risks, and the rewards.

**Skill levels:** Beginner to advanced routes available.
**Group sizes:** Small groups for a focused, personal experience.
**What's included:** Full gear briefing, safety protocols, and post-climb support.

You bring the drive. We handle the rest.
props.imageAlt: a group of people standing on top of a snow covered slope

items.q: What gear do I need to start high-altitude climbing?
items.a: Start with the essentials: insulated layering system, technical mountaineering boots, crampons, an ice axe, and a helmet. We break down exactly what to pack in our Summit Gear Guide.

items.q: How do I train for a summit attempt?
items.a: A solid base of cardiovascular fitness, leg strength, and weighted pack hikes is key. Our training plans ramp up progressively over 12–16 weeks to get you summit-ready.

items.q: How do I handle altitude sickness?
items.a: Acclimatize gradually — follow the 'climb high, sleep low' rule. Stay hydrated, ascend slowly, and give your body time to adjust. If symptoms worsen, descend immediately.
